fees: Government Colleges: GHC 1,500 - GHC 2,500 per year
Private Colleges: GHC 4,000 - GHC 6,000 per year
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 660 per year
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects (Science, General Arts, or Home Economics)
Overall aggregate of 36 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; must pass selection interview and medical examination
level_note: Government nursing colleges charge significantly lower fees due to state subsidies.
Graduates must pass the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana licensure exam to practice.

fees: Government Colleges: GHC 1,500 - GHC 2,500 per year
Private Colleges: GHC 4,000 - GHC 6,000 per year
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 660 per year
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 36 or better (WASSCE); females only at most colleges
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects
Overall aggregate of 36 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; most colleges admit females only; medical examination required
level_note: Registered Midwifery is offered as a standalone diploma at most government nursing and midwifery training colleges.
Graduates are licenced by the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana upon passing the qualifying examination.

fees: Government Colleges: GHC 1,500 - GHC 2,500 per year
Private Colleges: GHC 4,000 - GHC 6,000 per year
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 660 per year
mode: Placements at psychiatric hospitals and community mental health units
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects
Overall aggregate of 36 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; medical and psychological fitness required
level_note: Offered primarily at Accra Psychiatric Hospital School of Nursing and select government colleges.
Graduates play a key role in addressing Ghana's growing mental health workforce gap.

fees: Government Colleges: GHC 1,500 - GHC 2,500 per year
Private Colleges: GHC 4,000 - GHC 6,000 per year
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 660 per year
mode: Community field postings and health outreach
Clinic and district health facility rotations
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects
Overall aggregate of 36 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; fitness for community deployment required
level_note: Community Health Nursing is central to Ghana Health Service's community-based primary healthcare delivery.
Graduates are frequently posted to rural and underserved communities across all regions of Ghana.

fees: Government Colleges: GHC 800 - GHC 1,500 per year
Private Colleges: GHC 2,500 - GHC 4,500 per year
Additional levies: approx. GHC 400 per year
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 40 or better (WASSCE); passes accepted in elective subjects
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Passes (A1-E8) in three elective subjects
Overall aggregate of 40 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; medical fitness required
level_note: The NAC certificate is an entry-level clinical qualification that can be topped up to a Diploma in Nursing (2 years) or BSc Nursing (3 years).
Accredited by the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana and regulated under the Ghana Health Service.

fees: Government Colleges: GHC 800 - GHC 1,500 per year
Private Colleges: GHC 2,500 - GHC 4,000 per year
Additional levies: approx. GHC 400 per year
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Passes (A1-E8) in three elective subjects
Overall aggregate of 40 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; suitable for candidates with General Arts or General Agriculture background
level_note: The NAP certificate focuses on disease prevention and community environmental health, distinct from clinical bedside nursing.
Holders may top up to diploma or degree programmes after one year of rotation or internship.

fees: Post-Basic Diploma: GHC 3,000 - GHC 5,000 per year
BSc Top-Up (if applicable): GHC 5,000 - GHC 8,000 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150 - GHC 220
mode: Specialist placements at eye hospitals and outreach programmes
entry_requirements: Must hold a Diploma in General Nursing (RGN), Midwifery, or equivalent registered qualification
Valid PIN with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana
Minimum 3 years post-qualification work experience in relevant field
Currently in active service with a recognised health facility
level_note: Offered at the Ophthalmic Nursing School, Accra, one of the few specialist eye nursing training centres in West Africa.
Graduates play a critical role in Ghana's national eye health programme targeting preventable blindness.

fees: Post-Basic Diploma: GHC 3,000 - GHC 5,500 per year
BSc Top-Up: GHC 5,000 - GHC 8,500 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150 - GHC 220
mode: Advanced clinical simulations and ICU rotations
Attachments at teaching hospital critical care units
entry_requirements: Must hold a Diploma in General Nursing (RGN), Midwifery, or equivalent registered qualification
Valid PIN with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana
Minimum 3 years post-qualification work experience
Currently in active service with a recognised health facility
level_note: Offered at the School of Peri-Operative and Critical Care Nursing, Korle-Bu, affiliated with the University of Cape Coast.
One of the most competitive post-basic nursing specialisations in Ghana due to high ICU demand in teaching hospitals.

fees: Post-Basic Diploma: GHC 3,000 - GHC 5,500 per year
BSc Top-Up: GHC 5,000 - GHC 8,500 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150 - GHC 220
entry_requirements: Must hold a Diploma in General Nursing (RGN), Midwifery, or Psychiatry
Valid PIN with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana
Minimum 3 years post-qualification work experience
Currently in active service with a recognised health facility
level_note: Offered at the School of Peri-Operative and Critical Care Nursing, Korle-Bu, the only dedicated peri-operative nursing school in Ghana.
Advanced diploma holders may apply for a BSc top-up programme, requiring a further two semesters of study.

fees: Post-Basic Diploma: GHC 3,000 - GHC 5,000 per year
BSc Top-Up: GHC 5,000 - GHC 8,000 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150 - GHC 220
mode: Public health research and community projects
entry_requirements: Must hold a Diploma in General Nursing (RGN) or equivalent registered qualification
Valid PIN with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana
Minimum 3 years post-qualification work experience
Currently in active service with a recognised health or public health facility
level_note: Public Health Nursing post-basic training is offered at select nursing colleges affiliated with public health institutions in Ghana.
Graduates are eligible for senior roles within the Ghana Health Service and international health organisations.

career: Ear, Nose and Throat Theatre Nurse

fees: Post-Basic Diploma: GHC 3,000 - GHC 5,000 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150 - GHC 220
mode: Clinical rotations at ENT departments of teaching hospitals
Specialist ENT outpatient and theatre placements
entry_requirements: Must hold a Diploma in General Nursing (RGN) or equivalent registered qualification
Valid PIN with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana
Minimum 3 years post-qualification work experience in relevant field
Currently in active service with a recognised health facility
level_note: ENT Nursing is a rare and highly specialised post-basic programme offered at a limited number of accredited centres in Ghana.
Graduates address a critical shortage of ear, nose and throat specialist nurses across Ghana's public hospitals.

fees: Government Colleges: GHC 1,800 - GHC 3,000 per year
Private Colleges: GHC 4,000 - GHC 6,500 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150 - GHC 220
mode: Theory combined with obstetric clinical placements
Supervised deliveries and neonatal care rotations
entry_requirements: Must hold a Diploma in General Nursing (RGN) or equivalent registered nursing qualification
Valid PIN with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana
Minimum 1-2 years post-qualification work experience
Pass entrance examination conducted by the admitting college
level_note: Post Basic Midwifery is designed for qualified registered nurses who did not initially train as midwives but wish to obtain the additional midwifery qualification.
Successful graduates are registered as midwives with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Ghana and can practise both nursing and midwifery.
